title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Jeremy Claude and Francis continue to discuss their financial desperation, contemplating whether they can borrow money from a younger relative. Francis displays a ruthless determination to secure funds, even suggesting she might resort to robbing a bank.

In a separate setting, Rolly Claude and Lynn MacIntyre discuss the economic shifts following the war. Rolly observes that while farmers profited during the conflict, the era of large-scale farming is changing. He mentions that Mrs. Gordon intends to spend a large sum of money on a fur coat in London. Rolly describes Mrs. Gordon to Lynn as beautiful and seemingly innocent, though he suggests she may appear slow due to her thick accent and careful use of literary references. He also notes that her brother, David, seems potentially underhanded.

Rolly and Lynn discuss their personal lives and the "topsy-turvy" nature of post-war roles. While Rolly stayed home during the war, Lynn served in Egypt, North Africa, and Sicily, experiencing combat. Despite Lynn feeling disappointment regarding Rolly's perceived emotional guardedness, the two agree to marry in June and settle at Longshu Residence. Lynn reflects on how the war has fundamentally changed her identity.

At a party hosted by Uncle Lionel and Aunt Kathy, the guests observe the family dynamics. Dr. Claude struggles to suppress his anger while maintaining forced politeness. His brother, Leonel Claude, is a thin man with gray hair who lacks a lawyer's composure; he is characterized as irritable, unpredictable, and passionate about historical herbal research. Leonel finds it difficult to tolerate his wife's capricious personality.
